description_long: This is a large, open-topped wooden tub with an octagonal shape, constructed from thick vertical wooden staves held together by two prominent horizontal metal bands. The interior is filled with water, visible as a calm, blue surface just below the rim. The wood appears weathered and slightly uneven, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The metal bands are slightly rusted, adding to the rustic and utilitarian appearance. The tub has no visible handles or lid, and the staves are tightly fitted, forming a sturdy, watertight container. Its size and construction suggest it could be used for holding water, washing, or as a decorative feature in an urban or garden setting.
description: A large, open wooden tub with metal bands, filled with water and showing a rustic, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal wooden tub, water surface, and both metal bands clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the wood grain and the alignment of the metal bands.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the depth of the tub and the evenness of the staves.
description_view_3: Rear view with a clear look at the back staves and the continuous metal banding.
